_rebaseline_2026_07_11_portable_update_e: Portable update auto-extract fix (community report, 0.9.0 Beta 3). updates.py 611->653 (+42): new extract_portable_update(zip_path, dest_dir, *, max_total=None, max_ratio=None) -- a downloaded portable-update .zip was previously left as a bare unopened archive with no in-app way to act on it; this extracts it via safe_archive.open_zip (decompression-bomb limits threaded through as real parameters, not module-constant monkeypatching, so tests can override them deterministically) plus an explicit zip-slip guard (every member's resolved extraction path must stay inside dest_dir) as defense in depth, even though this only ever runs against Quill's own HTTPS+trusted-host-verified release asset. main_frame.py 28078->28119 (+41): _offer_post_download_actions gains an `extractable = target.suffix.lower() == '.zip'` branch alongside the existing `runnable` (.exe/.msi) one -- a portable .zip download previously only ever offered 'Open folder'/'Close' (never 'Install now', since that check only recognized installer extensions), so a portable user had to find and unzip the update themselves with zero in-app help. The new 'Extract now' button (new _extract_and_reveal_portable_update method) extracts to a ready-to-run sibling folder and reveals that instead of the raw archive. Does not attempt to replace the currently-running portable bundle in place (its own files may be locked while Quill is running) -- intentionally scoped to 'stop making the user manually unzip,' not a full self-replacing auto-updater. Cohesive single bug-fix, no unrelated growth.

All new pure logic lives in the new quill/core/code_folding.py (heading + fenced-code-block region detection, wx-free, unit-tested) and reuses quill/core/bookmarks.py's existing set_bookmark/bookmark_position for the numbered-quick-bookmark naming-convention feature (no new persistence schema). _comment: GATE-11 module-size budget. Each entry is the maximum allowed line count (Python splitlines) for that module. Budgets are a ratchet: they may only DECREASE as code is extracted (CQ-1), never increase. The gate fails when a tracked file grows past its budget, or when any untracked file exceeds the default cap (600) without an explicit, acknowledged entry here. Lower these numbers as main_frame.py and friends are decomposed.
